S&P 500 cap-weighted core — the 'idle = index' anchor in a low-VIX bull market. Invalidation: VIX breaks materially above ~22 or the chip-led selloff broadens into the whole tape (SPX decisively through recent support), at which point I'd stop adding and reassess.

S&P 500 equal-weight — deliberately underweights the mega-cap semis/AI names that just dropped ~6-13%, expressing caution on the exact source of this week's volatility while keeping broad beta. Invalidation: if breadth deteriorates and equal-weight starts materially lagging cap-weight (leadership re-narrows to a few winners), the de-concentration rationale weakens.

Nasdaq-100 growth beta, kept the smallest of the three precisely because tech/semis are the volatility source right now — I want participation in the AI-capex engine but underweight it here. Invalidation: a second leg down in semis (Broadcom/memory story worsening) would argue for trimming this before the broader ETFs.

Top up Nasdaq-100 growth beta now that semis/tech have stopped falling and VIX collapsed to 15.8 - the volatility that made me deliberately underweight QQQ in cycle 1 has resolved. Invalidation: a second leg down in semis (AVGO/memory story worsening) or VIX breaking back above ~22 would argue for trimming this first.

New small-cap (Russell 2000) sleeve for genuine breadth/de-concentration away from my mega-cap-heavy VOO/QQQ, consistent with my equal-weight RSP tilt; small-caps benefit from today's falling oil and subdued VIX. Invalidation: if breadth deteriorates and leadership re-narrows to mega-cap only (small-caps materially lagging), the de-concentration case weakens and I'd cut this.

Small oversold-bounce entry in liquid semis after last week's ~11% panic - the exact right-horizon setup my playbook endorses, not momentum-chasing - with a fresh dated catalyst (Broadcom-Apple 5-yr custom-silicon deal, 2026-07-06) and AI-capex thesis intact. Sized small (~12% NAV). Invalidation: the bounce fails and semis put in a lower low (SMH back through last week's trough), signaling the rout wasn't a one-off panic.
